definition: pip is Python's package installer that fetches and installs packages from PyPI; requirements.txt is a plain-text manifest listing the packages (and version specifiers) a project needs so the environment can be recreated with one command.
layman: pip is the app store for Python libraries, and requirements.txt is your shopping list — run one command and pip installs exactly what's on the list.
content:
## pip & requirements.txt

### pip basics

```bash
pip install openai                 # latest from PyPI
pip install "openai==1.40.0"       # exact version
pip install "langchain>=0.2,<0.3"  # range
pip install "openai[datalib]"      # optional extras
pip uninstall openai
pip list                            # what's installed
pip show openai                     # details for one package
```

### requirements.txt

A manifest, one requirement per line:

```text
openai==1.40.0
langchain>=0.2,<0.3
tiktoken
-r dev-requirements.txt   # include another file
```

Install everything with `pip install -r requirements.txt`.

### Version specifiers

| Specifier | Meaning |
|-----------|---------|
| `==1.40.0` | exactly this version (most reproducible) |
| `>=0.2,<0.3` | a compatible range |
| `~=1.4.0` | compatible release (>=1.4.0, <1.5.0) |
| (none) | latest available — least reproducible |

### freeze vs curated lists

`pip freeze > requirements.txt` captures *every* installed package at exact versions, including transitive dependencies. It's reproducible but noisy. A common practice is to hand-curate the **direct** dependencies and generate a fully-pinned lock separately (see Lockfiles & Reproducibility).

### Why this matters for Gen AI

Gen AI libraries move fast and frequently make breaking changes. Pinning `openai`, `langchain`, `tiktoken`, etc. in requirements.txt ensures the code that worked yesterday still works today and on every teammate's machine and in CI.
keyPoints: requirements.txt lists dependencies with version specifiers; install with pip install -r.
== pins exactly (most reproducible); ranges trade reproducibility for flexibility.
pip freeze captures all installed versions including transitive deps.
Pinning fast-moving Gen AI libraries prevents 'works on my machine' breakage.
